title: Anion Gap Blood Test: MedlinePlus Medical Test
text:
he blood is called acidosis . Too little acid in your blood is called alkalosis . Both conditions can be serious.Â 
Why do I need an anion gap blood test? 
Your health care provider may order an anion gap blood test if you have symptoms of acidosis or alkalosis. Symptoms may vary depending on the underlying disease or condition.
Symptoms of acidosis may include:
Shortness of breath 
Vomiting 
Confusion 
Fatigue 
Tachycardia (very fast heartbeat) 
Symptoms of alkalosis may include:
Arrhythmia (a problem with the rate or rhythm of your heartbeat) 
Irritability 
Hand tremor 
Muscle twitching 
Nausea, vomiting 
Numbness or tingling in the face, hands, or feet 
What happens during an anion gap blood test? 
The anion gap test is a calculation that's done with the results of an electrolyte panel, which is a blood test.
During a blood test, a health care professional will take a blood sample from a vein in your arm, using a small needle. After the needle is inserted, a small amount of blood will be collected into a test tube. You may feel a little sting when the needle goes in or out. This usually takes less than five minutes.
Will I need to do anything to prepare for the test? 
No special preparation is necessary for an anion gap blood test. If your provider has ordered more tests on your blood sample, you may need to fast (not eat or drink) for several hours before the test. Your pro
